channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Uber Accelerates Climate Efforts with Electric Vehicle Updates and Mentorship Program
item.description: Uber announces climate-focused updates, including electric vehicle preferences and AI assistant, to achieve zero-emissions platform by 2040
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Aryaka brings CASB into unified SASE fold
item.description: Aryaka continues to build out its SASE platform, adding new capabilities aimed at enhancing security and simplifying the adoption process for enterprises that want to consolidate their network and security deployments. At the heart of this update is Aryaka’s new cloud access security broker (CASB).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Día Rosa Tecnológico: el movimiento mundial de TI del CIO de Estée Lauder para financiar la investigación del cáncer de mama
item.description: Cuando se unió a The Estée Lauder Companies en 2017, Michael Smith reconoció una forma en que podría ayudar a marcar la diferencia de una manera diferente a sus típicas tareas de CIO: crear conciencia sobre el cáncer de mama.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Many C-suite execs have lost confidence in IT, including CIOs
item.description: Some bad news for CIOs: Many C-suite leaders, including IT leaders themselves, have less confidence in your department’s ability to get the job done than they did a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Headhunted? That dream job could be a trap
item.description: Cybercriminals are increasingly targeting private accounts on LinkedIn and WhatsApp because they are less secure than business accounts, according to Europe cybersecurity agency Enisa’s latest threat landscape report.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: SAP launches collaborative AI agents, adds Knowledge Graph
item.description: SAP’s promised collaboration between its AI copilot, Joule, and other agents will become reality in the fourth quarter of 2024, the company announced at its 2024 TechEd conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This was the most disturbing part of Elon Musk’s Trump rally speech
item.description: Elon Musk achieved platinum meme status and got ridiculed on Saturday Night Live for gleefully leaping onstage at a Trump rally over the weekend. But a photo of Trump glowering as the Tesla tycoon flung himself skyward was far from Musk’s most egregious moment at that rally.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Google ordered to open up Play app store to competition
item.description: A U.S. judge on Monday ordered Alphabet’s Google to overhaul its mobile app business to give Android users more options to download apps and to pay for transactions within them, following a jury verdict last year for Fortnite maker Epic Games. The injunction by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Control the path and power of hurricanes like Milton? Forget it, scientists say
item.description: In the wake of hurricanes that have disrupted lives for millions in the U.S., some people may wonder if extreme weather events could be somehow controlled.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling And Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal judge orders Google to open its Android app store to competition
item.description: A federal judge has ordered Google to tear down the digital walls shielding its Android app store from competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Unicorns wanted: Navigating the Product Owner shortage in IT
item.description: This article was co-authored by Kira Kessel, Associate at Metis Strategy. You’ve seen the virtues of transforming from a project to a product operating model: value-driven work, delivered by dedicated teams rather than through projects led by disparate team members.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: EU ruling clamps down on Meta’s use of personal data for ads
item.description: The European Court of Justice has decided that Facebook owner Meta must minimize the amount of personal data it uses for personalized ads, the BBC reports . The decision from the EU’s top court means that only a small part of Meta’s data collection can be used for advertisements.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear appeal from Elon Musk’s X platform over warrant in Trump case
item.description: The Supreme Court says it won't hear an appeal from the social media platform X over a search warrant prosecutors obtained in the election-interference case against former President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up challenge to Jack Smith’s Trump Twitter data access
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to take up a challenge by social platform X, formerly known as Twitter, to court rulings that forced the platform to turn over data on former President Trump’s account to special counsel Jack Smith.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI-powered transcription leaks workplace secrets
item.description: Alex Bilzerian shared on X that the Otter AI platform shared confidential information, The Washington Post first reported.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Node.js previews network inspection support
item.description: Node.js v20.18.0, a just-introduced update to the Long-Term Support (LTS) version of the popular asynchronous, event-driven JavaScript runtime, features experimental network inspection support. Introduced October 3, Node.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google’s ad-tech antitrust trial: Wrapping up the case for the defense
item.description: And now we wait. Last week, Google did its best to refute the US Department of Justice’s case that the company has built a series of monopolies in the markets for ad serving, ad exchanges, and ad networks, and the DOJ made its rebuttal. Closing arguments will be heard Nov.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Social media suspensions may be linked to misinformation, not platform bias: Study
item.description: Social media platforms’ suspensions of accounts may not be rooted in political biases, but rather certain political groups’ tendency to share misinformation, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Open AI Unveils Canvas: A New ChatGPT Interface for Developers
item.description: Open AI introduces Canvas, a beta version of ChatGPT interface designed specifically for developers with advanced coding features and shortcuts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meet Spot, the LAPD's new crimefighting robot dog
item.description: The futuristic four-legged machine, which boasts the size and agility of a 70-pound golden retriever, has been met with both fascination and concern.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, The Hill

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Google said Friday it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The creepy yet helpful humanoid robot ready to move into your home
item.description: A robot that stands more than 5 feet tall and weighs 66 pounds is well-equipped for household tasks, like carrying up to 44 pounds during its run time of two to four hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why cloud security outranks cost and scalability
item.description: According to a study by Akamai Technologies, 87% of digital-native businesses (which seems to be a term specific to Asia/Pacific) now prioritize security over cost and scalability when selecting a cloud provider.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.94 improves file search
item.description: The September 2024 release of Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor, version 1.94, features improvements for finding files using the File Explorer. The upgrade also introduces the ability to run Python tests with coverage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How data brokers are fueling elder fraud in America
item.description: Criminals are increasingly turning to data brokers to provide access to private information that can be used to improve their scamming schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenAI updates API with model distillation, prompt caching abilities
item.description: In what can only be seen as OpenAI’s efforts to catch up with rivals, the ChatGPT-maker released several updates to its API to help ease the development of generative AI-based applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI previews Realtime API for speech-to-speech apps
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a public beta of the Realtime API, an API that allows paid developers to build low-latency, multi-modal experiences including text and speech in apps.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo
item.description: The new executive assistant director for cybersecurity at CISA details how sharing threat intelligence more broadly pays off. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
